metadata.form_title: Form 1065 Schedule K / K-1 Line 14a — Self-Employment Earnings (SECA) (TY2025)

facts.label: Partner type (general / limited / llc_member)
facts.notes: Starting point: general → fixed active; limited / llc_member → classification required (Decision 2: LLC members ride the same active/passive axis).

facts.label: SE classification (§1402(a)(13): active / passive / undetermined)
facts.notes: The preparer's functional-analysis call. Drives every component (§3). Default undetermined on create; general is forced active.

facts.label: Classification basis (functional-analysis note)
facts.notes: Optional free-text audit trail supporting the characterization (Whittington defense).

facts.label: Distributive share of ordinary business income (box 1)
facts.notes: The ordinary-income SE base for this partner (may be negative — a distributive-share loss). The base's full worksheet definition is out of scope (§14).

facts.label: Guaranteed payment for services (§707(c))
facts.notes: SE for EVERY partner type (§1402(a)(13) services carve-back).

facts.label: Guaranteed payment for use of capital (§707(c))
facts.notes: SE iff active (Decision 4); excluded if passive/limited (Decision 3).

facts.label: Individual partner? (non-individuals get no box 14a)
facts.notes: §14.5 guard: a non-individual partner (Partner.is_individual = False) generally gets no box 14a (full guard deferred to the implementation spec).

facts.label: K-1 box 14a — partner net earnings/(loss) from self-employment
facts.notes: OUTPUT. Per-partner: (active? box1 with loss symmetry : 0) + gp_services + (active? gp_capital : 0).

facts.label: Schedule K line 14a — entity total (Σ per-partner box 14a)
facts.notes: OUTPUT. Derived bottom-up = Σ per-partner K-1 box 14a (RECON-14A).

rules.title: Classification drives component treatment
rules.description: §3 determination model. §1402(a)(13) resolved by functional analysis (Soroban).
rules.formula: Per-partner se_classification ∈ {active, passive, undetermined} is the preparer's §1402(a)(13) functional-analysis call (does the partner function as a general/active partner or a limited/passive investor?). general → fixed active (settled). limited / llc_member → classification required (Decision 2: LLC members ride the same active/passive axis per Renkemeyer). The classification drives R-SE-DSHARE and R-SE-GPCAP (§3 table).

authorities.title: IRC §1402 — Net Earnings From Self-Employment (partner distributive share; rental/portfolio exclusions; limited-partner exclusion)

authorities.citation: Renkemeyer 136 T.C. 137 (2011); Soroban 161 T.C. No. 12 (2023) & T.C. Memo. 2025-52; Denham T.C. Memo. 2024-114; contra Sirius No. 24-60240 (5th Cir. 2026)
authorities.relevance_note: Renkemeyer/Soroban functional analysis (active vs. limited 'as such')

rules.title: Distributive share (box 1) → SE per classification
rules.description: §4 component table, row 1. Contested (preparer) for limited/llc; auto for general.
rules.formula: Distributive share of ordinary business income (box 1) is SE iff active (active/general, or undetermined defaulted to active per R-SE-DEFAULT); excluded if passive/limited. §1402(a) + §702(a)(8) + Reg §1.1402(a)-1(a)(2).

authorities.title: IRC §1402 — Net Earnings From Self-Employment (partner distributive share; rental/portfolio exclusions; limited-partner exclusion)
authorities.relevance_note: §1402(a) distributive share of §702(a)(8) income in the SE base

authorities.title: IRC §702 — Income and Credits of Partner (distributive share; character conduit)
Treas. Reg. §1.1402(a)-1 — Definition of net earnings from self-employment (distributive share; guaranteed payments for services or use of capital)

rules.title: Guaranteed payment for services → SE (all partner types)
rules.description: §4 component table, row 2. Automatic, always included.
rules.formula: Guaranteed payment for services (§707(c)) → SE for EVERY partner type, automatic. The §1402(a)(13) carve-back retains services GP even for a limited/passive partner. Reg §1.1402(a)-1(b). Not reduced by a distributive-share loss (§4a).

authorities.title: IRC §707(c) — Guaranteed Payments (services or use of capital)
IRC §1402 — Net Earnings From Self-Employment (partner distributive share; rental/portfolio exclusions; limited-partner exclusion)
authorities.relevance_note: §1402(a)(13) carve-back retains services GP for all partner types

authorities.title: Treas. Reg. §1.1402(a)-1 — Definition of net earnings from self-employment (distributive share; guaranteed payments for services or use of capital)
authorities.relevance_note: Reg §1.1402(a)-1(b) payments for services are business income

rules.title: Guaranteed payment for capital → SE iff active
rules.formula: Guaranteed payment for the use of capital (§707(c)) → SE iff active; excluded if passive/limited. Decision 4: active/general capital GP IS SE (Reg §1.1402(a)-1(b), use of capital). Decision 3: passive/limited capital GP is EXCLUDED (§1402(a)(13) services-only carve-back).

authorities.title: Treas. Reg. §1.1402(a)-1 — Definition of net earnings from self-employment (distributive share; guaranteed payments for services or use of capital)
authorities.relevance_note: Reg §1.1402(a)-1(b) use of capital → business income (Decision 4)

authorities.title: IRC §1402 — Net Earnings From Self-Employment (partner distributive share; rental/portfolio exclusions; limited-partner exclusion)
authorities.relevance_note: §1402(a)(13) services-only carve-back (capital GP excluded if passive)

rules.title: Undetermined → active safety net (Decision 1)
rules.formula: When se_classification = undetermined AND the partner has nonzero allocated ordinary income: (a) fire D_SE_UNDET, and (b) apply the safety-net default = treat as ACTIVE (INCLUDE) so the return computes. Rationale: GA is 11th Cir. (no controlling appellate precedent) → the Tax Court functional posture is operative; include-on-undetermined never silently understates SE (the penalty-drawing direction). A safety net, NOT a substitute for the real call.

authorities.title: Limited-partner SE functional-analysis case line (§1402(a)(13) 'limited partner, as such')
authorities.citation: Renkemeyer 136 T.C. 137 (2011); Soroban 161 T.C. No. 12 (2023) & T.C. Memo. 2025-52; Denham T.C. Memo. 2024-114; contra Sirius No. 24-60240 (5th Cir. 2026)
authorities.relevance_note: 11th Cir. (GA) unresolved → Tax Court functional posture → active default

authorities.title: IRC §1402 — Net Earnings From Self-Employment (partner distributive share; rental/portfolio exclusions; limited-partner exclusion)
authorities.relevance_note: §1402(a)(13) — the provision whose application is undetermined

rules.title: Rental real estate (box 2) excluded from SE
rules.formula: Rental real estate (box 2) is NOT SE for any partner type. §1402(a)(1); Reg §1.1402(a)-4 (excluded unless received in the course of a trade or business as a real-estate dealer).

authorities.title: IRC §1402 — Net Earnings From Self-Employment (partner distributive share; rental/portfolio exclusions; limited-partner exclusion)
Treas. Reg. §1.1402(a)-4 — Rentals from real estate (excluded from SE)
authorities.relevance_note: Reg §1.1402(a)-4 rentals excluded unless real-estate dealer

rules.title: Portfolio (boxes 5/6/8/9) excluded from SE
rules.formula: Portfolio income — interest / dividends / capital gains (boxes 5/6/8/9) — is NOT SE for any partner type. §1402(a)(2) (dividends/interest, non-dealer); §1402(a)(3) (capital gains). Character conduit §702(b).

authorities.title: IRC §1402 — Net Earnings From Self-Employment (partner distributive share; rental/portfolio exclusions; limited-partner exclusion)

rules.title: Loss symmetry
rules.description: §4a losses — explicit symmetry; writes the per-partner box 14a.
rules.formula: §4a: an ACTIVE partner (incl. undetermined→active) with a negative distributive-share base flows a NEGATIVE 14a (SE loss) — you cannot include gains but drop losses. A PASSIVE partner's distributive share is excluded whether gain OR loss. Guaranteed payments for services are NOT reduced by a distributive-share loss. Net per-partner 14a = (active? box1 : 0) + gp_services + (active? gp_capital : 0).

authorities.title: IRC §1402 — Net Earnings From Self-Employment (partner distributive share; rental/portfolio exclusions; limited-partner exclusion)
authorities.relevance_note: §1402(a) 'income OR loss' — symmetry for active partners

authorities.title: 2025 Instructions for Form 1065 — Worksheet for Figuring Net Earnings (Loss) From Self-Employment (p.45)
authorities.citation: Instructions for Form 1065 (2025), SE Worksheet, lines 1a-5 (p.45)
authorities.relevance_note: SE Worksheet line 1a base can be negative

rules.title: Entity Sch K 14a derived bottom-up = Σ per-partner
rules.description: §9 RECON-14A. Bottom-up derivation is the spec requirement.
rules.formula: Entity Schedule K line 14a is derived BOTTOM-UP = Σ (per-partner K-1 box 14a), NOT computed separately (replaces the independent K14a = K1 + K4c path). Matches the IRS SE Worksheet (i1065 2025 p.45): entity line 14a = line 3c + line 4c. RECON-14A enforces equality; a break fires D_SE_RECON.

authorities.title: 2025 Instructions for Form 1065 — Worksheet for Figuring Net Earnings (Loss) From Self-Employment (p.45)
authorities.citation: Instructions for Form 1065 (2025), SE Worksheet, lines 1a-5 (p.45)

line_map.description: Schedule K, line 14a — Net earnings (loss) from self-employment (entity total = Σ per-partner K-1 box 14a)
line_map.notes: Derived bottom-up (RECON-14A). Replaces the independent K14a = K1 + K4c path.

line_map.description: Schedule K-1, box 14, code A — Partner's net earnings (loss) from self-employment
line_map.notes: Per-partner. 14b/14c (gross farming / nonfarm) are OUT OF SCOPE (§14). destination_form SCHEDULE_SE is the FUTURE flow-through gated by FLOW-14A-SE (inactive).

diagnostics.title: Guaranteed-payment character — capital vs. services (Whittington risk)
diagnostics.condition: se_gp_capital > 0 AND se_gp_services == 0 for an active partner
diagnostics.message: Confirm the capital/services split of this guaranteed payment is substantiated. The IRS can recharacterize a 'capital' guaranteed payment as being for services (Whittington / Seismic Support Services), which changes its SE treatment. Concrete threshold: capital GP present with zero services GP for an active partner.

diagnostics.title: Reconciliation break — entity 14a ≠ Σ per-partner 14a
diagnostics.condition: entity Sch K line 14a != Σ per-partner K-1 box 14a
diagnostics.message: Reconciliation break: entity Schedule K line 14a must equal the sum of the per-partner K-1 box 14a amounts (RECON-14A). Entity 14a is derived bottom-up from the per-partner determinations — it is not computed separately.

diagnostics.title: SE classification undetermined — preparer determination required
diagnostics.condition: se_partner_type in (limited, llc_member) AND se_box1_ordinary != 0 AND se_classification == 'undetermined'
diagnostics.message: Preparer determination required under §1402(a)(13): this partner's active/passive status is unsettled and circuit-dependent (Renkemeyer / Soroban functional analysis; the 11th Circuit / Georgia is unresolved). The safety-net default — treat as ACTIVE and INCLUDE the distributive share — has been applied so the return computes. Confirm the classification: a genuinely passive investor should be set to 'passive' so they are not over-taxed.

tests.scenario_name: T2 — active limited partner = general treatment
T3 — passive limited partner, services GP only
T5 — passive LLC member, fixes silent full-inclusion
T7 — RECON-14A: entity 14a = Σ per-partner box 14a
T8 — active capital GP only, Whittington flag

authority_sources.title: Limited-partner SE functional-analysis case line (§1402(a)(13) 'limited partner, as such')
authority_sources.citation: Renkemeyer 136 T.C. 137 (2011); Soroban 161 T.C. No. 12 (2023) & T.C. Memo. 2025-52; Denham T.C. Memo. 2024-114; contra Sirius No. 24-60240 (5th Cir. 2026)
authority_sources.issuer: U.S. Tax Court; U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit

excerpts.excerpt_label: Denham, T.C. Memo. 2024-114 (Dec 23 2024) — functional analysis applied
excerpts.excerpt_text: The functional-analysis inquiry was applied to a private-equity fund's limited partners under §1402(a)(13).
excerpts.summary_text: Denham: extends the functional analysis to a PE-fund limited-partner structure.

excerpts.excerpt_label: Renkemeyer, 136 T.C. 137 (2011) — functional analysis established
excerpts.excerpt_text: Partners in a law-firm LLP were not 'limited partners, as such' under §1402(a)(13); their distributive shares arising from performing legal services for the partnership were net earnings from self-employment — the functional-analysis line.
excerpts.summary_text: Renkemeyer: a state-law limited-liability label does not exclude a partner whose share derives from services rendered.

excerpts.excerpt_label: Sirius (CONTRA), No. 24-60240 (5th Cir. Jan 16 2026) — state-law text test
excerpts.excerpt_text: Applies a state-law text test to §1402(a)(13); binds Louisiana, Mississippi, and Texas only. The 11th Circuit (Georgia) is unresolved, so this holding does not bind GA clients.
excerpts.summary_text: Sirius (5th Cir.): contra authority; binds LA/MS/TX only — GA/11th Cir. unresolved (drives the §6 include-on-undetermined default).

excerpts.excerpt_label: Soroban I, 161 T.C. No. 12 (Nov 28 2023) — functional test required
excerpts.excerpt_text: §1402(a)(13)'s 'limited partner, as such' exclusion requires a functional-analysis inquiry into the partner's role; a partner's state-law 'limited partner' status is not dispositive of the exclusion.
excerpts.summary_text: Soroban I: the functional test is required — the label alone does not control the §1402(a)(13) exclusion.

excerpts.excerpt_label: Soroban II, T.C. Memo. 2025-52 (May 28 2025) — test applied, LPs taxable
excerpts.excerpt_text: Applying the functional test, the limited partners' distributive shares were held includible (taxable) in net earnings from self-employment.
excerpts.summary_text: Soroban II: on the facts, the limited partners were active — shares included in SE.

authority_sources.title: IRC §1402 — Net Earnings From Self-Employment (partner distributive share; rental/portfolio exclusions; limited-partner exclusion)

excerpts.excerpt_label: §1402(a) — distributive share in the SE base
excerpts.excerpt_text: the gross income derived by an individual from any trade or business carried on by such individual, less the deductions allowed by this subtitle which are attributable to such trade or business, plus his distributive share (whether or not distributed) of income or loss described in section 702(a)(8)
excerpts.summary_text: Net earnings from self-employment include the partner's distributive share (income OR loss) of §702(a)(8) trade-or-business income.

excerpts.excerpt_label: §1402(a) — Net earnings from self-employment
excerpts.excerpt_text: Net earnings from self-employment means gross income derived from any trade or business carried on by the individual, less allowable deductions attributable to such trade or business, plus the distributive share (whether or not distributed) of income from any trade or business of a partnership of which the individual is a member. Exclusions: (1) rental from real estate (§1402(a)(1)) unless a real estate dealer, (2) dividends and interest (unless a dealer), (3) gain or loss from disposition of property that is not inventory, (4) net earnings of less than $400. Limited partners receive guaranteed payments only as SE income.
excerpts.summary_text: SE income = business net income + partnership distributive share. Excludes rents, dividends, capital gains.

excerpts.excerpt_label: §1402(a) partner's distributive share is SE earnings
excerpts.excerpt_text: The term 'net earnings from self-employment' means … the distributive share (whether or not distributed) of income or loss … from any trade or business carried on by a partnership of which he is a member … there shall be excluded the distributive share of any item of income or loss of a limited partner, as such, other than guaranteed payments … for services actually rendered.
excerpts.summary_text: Partner's trade/business distributive share = SE earnings (box 14A → Sch SE); a limited partner excludes all but guaranteed payments for services.

excerpts.excerpt_label: §1402(a)(1) — rentals from real estate excluded
excerpts.excerpt_text: there shall be excluded rentals from real estate and from personal property leased with the real estate (including such rentals paid in crop shares, and including payments under section 1233(a)(2) of the Food Security Act of 1985 (16 U.S.C. 3833(a)(2)) to individuals receiving benefits under section 202 or 223 of the Social Security Act) together with the deductions attributable thereto, unless such rentals are received in the course of a trade or business as a real estate dealer
excerpts.summary_text: Rental real estate (box 2) is excluded from SE unless received as a real-estate dealer.

excerpts.excerpt_label: §1402(a)(13) — limited-partner exclusion (services-only carve-back)
excerpts.excerpt_text: there shall be excluded the distributive share of any item of income or loss of a limited partner, as such, other than guaranteed payments described in section 707(c) to that partner for services actually rendered to or on behalf of the partnership to the extent that those payments are established to be in the nature of remuneration for those services
excerpts.summary_text: A limited partner 'as such' excludes the distributive share, EXCEPT §707(c) guaranteed payments for services — the carve-back is services-only (so capital GP of a limited partner is excluded).

excerpts.excerpt_label: §1402(a)(2)/(a)(3) — dividends, interest, and capital gains excluded
excerpts.excerpt_text: (2) there shall be excluded dividends on any share of stock, and interest on any bond, debenture, note, or certificate, or other evidence of indebtedness, issued with interest coupons or in registered form by any corporation (including one issued by a government or political subdivision thereof), unless such dividends and interest are received in the course of a trade or business as a dealer in stocks or securities; (3) there shall be excluded any gain or loss— (A) which is considered as gain or loss from the sale or exchange of a capital asset
excerpts.summary_text: Portfolio interest/dividends (non-dealer) and capital gains (boxes 5/6/8/9) are excluded from SE.

excerpts.excerpt_label: §1402(a)(8) — housing/parsonage in net SE earnings
excerpts.excerpt_text: The rental value of a home furnished to a minister, or a rental allowance paid to a minister (including the value of meals and lodging), shall be included in computing net earnings from self-employment, notwithstanding the exclusion of such amounts from gross income under section 107.
excerpts.summary_text: §1402(a)(8): the §107-excluded housing/parsonage value is added back into the SE base.

excerpts.excerpt_label: §1402(e) — Form 4361 exemption from SE tax
excerpts.excerpt_text: A minister who is conscientiously opposed to, or because of religious principles opposed to, the acceptance of public insurance may file an application for an exemption from the tax imposed by chapter 2 with respect to services performed as a minister. An approved exemption is irrevocable.
excerpts.summary_text: §1402(e): an approved Form 4361 exempts ministerial earnings from SE tax; irrevocable.

authority_sources.title: IRC §702 — Income and Credits of Partner (distributive share; character conduit)

excerpts.excerpt_label: §702(a) — Separately stated items
excerpts.excerpt_text: In determining income tax, each partner shall take into account separately the partner's distributive share of: (1) short-term capital gains/losses, (2) long-term capital gains/losses, (3) §1231 gains/losses, (4) charitable contributions, (5) dividends eligible for special rates, (6) taxes described in §901 (foreign tax credit), (7) other items required to be stated separately, and (8) taxable income or loss exclusive of items above.
excerpts.summary_text: Partners separately account for cap gains/losses, §1231, charitable, foreign tax, and other items.

excerpts.excerpt_label: §702(a)(8) — taxable income/loss distributive share
excerpts.excerpt_text: taxable income or loss, exclusive of items requiring separate computation under other paragraphs of this subsection.
excerpts.summary_text: §702(a)(8) is the residual trade-or-business income/loss distributive share referenced by §1402(a).

excerpts.excerpt_label: §702(b) — character determined at the partnership level
excerpts.excerpt_text: The character of any item of income, gain, loss, deduction, or credit included in a partner's distributive share under paragraphs (1) through (7) of subsection (a) shall be determined as if such item were realized directly from the source from which realized by the partnership, or incurred in the same manner as incurred by the partnership.
excerpts.summary_text: Each item keeps its partnership-level character in the partner's hands (rental stays rental, portfolio stays portfolio).

authority_sources.title: IRC §707(c) — Guaranteed Payments (services or use of capital)

excerpts.excerpt_label: §707(c) — guaranteed payments for services or use of capital
excerpts.excerpt_text: To the extent determined without regard to the income of the partnership, payments to a partner for services or the use of capital shall be considered as made to one who is not a member of the partnership, but only for the purposes of section 61(a) (relating to gross income) and, subject to section 263, for purposes of section 162(a) (relating to trade or business expenses).
excerpts.summary_text: Guaranteed payments are for services OR the use of capital — the split that drives R-SE-GPSVC (always SE) vs. R-SE-GPCAP (SE iff active).

authority_sources.title: 2025 Instructions for Form 1065 — Worksheet for Figuring Net Earnings (Loss) From Self-Employment (p.45)
authority_sources.citation: Instructions for Form 1065 (2025), SE Worksheet, lines 1a-5 (p.45)

excerpts.excerpt_label: §1402(a)(13) split + entity 14a (lines 3b/4b/3c/4c → 14a)
excerpts.excerpt_text: Line 3b removes limited partners' distributive share; line 4b removes the part of line 4a (guaranteed payments) allocated to limited partners for other than services (their capital GP); services GP is retained on line 4c; general partners keep line 3c plus the full 4c. Entity Schedule K line 14a = line 3c + line 4c.
excerpts.summary_text: Entity 14a = 3c + 4c; the worksheet's mechanical 'limited partner' label at 3b/4b is what this spec replaces with se_classification.

excerpts.excerpt_label: SE Worksheet base (lines 1a-3a)
excerpts.excerpt_text: base = Sch K line 1 (1a) + certain rental RE, dealer/substantial-services only (1b) + other net rental / Sch K line 3c (1c) + Form 4797 Part II loss (1d) - Form 4797 Part II gain (2) = line 3a.
excerpts.summary_text: The ordinary-income SE base (NOT defined by this spec — §14 sub-spec): raw box 1 plus the conditional rental inclusions and the Form 4797 Part II adjustment.

authority_sources.title: Treas. Reg. §1.1402(a)-1 — Definition of net earnings from self-employment (distributive share; guaranteed payments for services or use of capital)

excerpts.excerpt_label: §1.1402(a)-1(a)(2) — distributive share of partnership trade/business
excerpts.excerpt_text: His distributive share (whether or not distributed), as determined under section 704, of the income (or minus the loss), described in section 702(a)(9) and as computed under section 703, from any trade or business carried on by any partnership of which he is a member.
excerpts.summary_text: The partner's distributive share of partnership trade-or-business income (or minus loss) is in the SE base.

excerpts.excerpt_label: §1.1402(a)-1(b) — payments for services or use of capital are business income
excerpts.excerpt_text: Gross income derived by an individual from a trade or business includes payments received by him from a partnership of which he is a member for services rendered to the partnership or for the use of capital by the partnership, to the extent the payments are determined without regard to the income of the partnership. However, such payments received from a partnership not engaged in a trade or business within the meaning of section 1402(c) and § 1.1402(c)-1 do not constitute gross income derived by an individual from a trade or business.
excerpts.summary_text: Guaranteed payments for services OR for the use of capital are trade-or-business gross income (→ SE) — the reg basis for Decision 4 (active capital GP is SE).

authority_sources.title: Treas. Reg. §1.1402(a)-4 — Rentals from real estate (excluded from SE)

excerpts.excerpt_label: §1.1402(a)-4(a) — rentals from real estate excluded (general rule)
excerpts.excerpt_text: Rentals from real estate and from personal property leased with the real estate (including such rentals paid in crop shares) and the deductions attributable thereto, unless such rentals are received by an individual in the course of a trade or business as a real-estate dealer, are excluded.
excerpts.summary_text: Rental real estate is excluded from SE unless the individual is a real-estate dealer.
